scenario_description: Two identical black SUVs drive side-by-side. The left one overtakes a motorcycle while the right one maintains its lane. Later, only one SUV is visible ahead of the motorcycle.

turns.text: Here's the start of the clip. Two black SUVs are driving next to each other on a highway.
turns.image_description: A daytime shot from a rear-facing camera on a moving vehicle. In the center frame, two identical matte-black luxury SUVs are driving side-by-side in adjacent lanes. The SUV in the left lane (Lane 2) is slightly ahead of the SUV in the right lane (Lane 3). Between them and slightly behind, a silver motorcycle is visible in Lane 2, just behind the left SUV. The road is dry asphalt with white dashed lane markers. The lighting is bright and even.

turns.text: Got it. They look like twins. Is that motorcycle trying to pass either of them?
Yeah, watch what happens here. The one on the left moves over.
turns.image_description: Same scene as before, but time has advanced by a few seconds. The SUV that was in the left lane (Lane 2) has now fully merged into the right lane (Lane 3), positioning itself directly in front of the other black SUV which has stayed in Lane 3. The silver motorcycle is now clearly visible in Lane 2, passing the gap where the first SUV used to be. The two SUVs are now bumper-to-bumper in the same lane, with the one that moved now leading the pair.

turns.text: Okay, so the left one cut in front of the right one. That must have been tight for the motorcyclist!
Fast forward a bit more. Now I only see one car ahead.
turns.image_description: A view further down the highway. The silver motorcycle is no longer in the frame. Only one matte-black SUV is visible in the distance, centered in the right-most lane (Lane 3). It is driving away from the camera. There are no other vehicles immediately surrounding it in the visible frame.

turns.text: Right, just one black SUV left in the shot. Hard to tell which one it is just from this angle since they're identical models.
Which of the original two is currently leading the pack? A) The one that started on the left B) The one that started on the right C) Neither, they are tied D) The motorcycle

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: In Turn 1, there are two SUVs: Left (L) and Right (R). In Turn 3, L merges into R's lane and positions itself directly in front of R. This establishes that L is now leading R. In Turn 5, only one SUV is visible ahead. Since L was established as being in front of R in the previous turn, and no new vehicles entered or overtook them, the single visible SUV must be L. Therefore, the one that started on the left is leading.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must track the spatial relationship change across turns (Left merging in front of Right) and map that state to the final image where only one entity remains visible. Without remembering the merge event in Turn 3, the user cannot distinguish between option A and B based solely on the final image.

_meta.scenario: Two identical black SUVs are driving side-by-side; the left one changes lanes to overtake a motorcycle, while the right one stays put. A few seconds later, only one black SUV is visible ahead. The user asks which of the original two (left or right) is currently leading the pack.
